etymology.description: 艸(艸) (cǎo, "grass; plant") semantic + 者(者) (zhě) phonetic -> related to writing or creating a significant work
etymology.interpretation: The character 著(著) (zhù, "write") combines the semantic 艸(艸) (cǎo, "grass; plant") with the phonetic 者(者) (zhě, "person responsible") to suggest writing as an act of creation, akin to cultivating plants. The prominently used phonetic 者 conveys the sound while the imagery implies bringing forth or producing something notable.
etymology.simplification: 着(着): The components 艹 and 日 were replaced with 䒑 and 目 to create a simpler, albeit non-standard, form that has been recorded from as early as the Tang dynasty.

examples.chinese: 他正在写一本书(他正在寫一本書)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài xiě yī běn shū.

examples.chinese: 这篇文章是著名作家所著(這篇文章是著名作家所著)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè piān wénzhāng shì zhùmíng zuòjiā suǒ zhù.
examples.english: This article is written by a famous author.

examples.chinese: 她正在著一部新小说(她正在著一部新小說)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài zhù yī bù xīn xiǎoshuō.
examples.english: She is currently writing a new novel.
